req.title: Vital signs validation (negative)
req.statement: An Observation with category vital-signs shall have value in clinically plausible range; out-of-range shall be flagged for review. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.
expected_outcomes: flag raised if out of range

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
pytest tests for FHIR Observation vital-signs range validation.

Requirement:
    An Observation with category vital-signs shall have value in a clinically
    plausible range; out-of-range values shall be flagged for review.
    Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements,
    and conflicting state transitions must be tested.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from enum import Enum
from typing import Optional

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models (production code would live elsewhere; defined here so the
# test file is self-contained and runnable with `pytest` directly)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class ObservationStatus(Enum):
    REGISTERED = "registered"
    PRELIMINARY = "preliminary"
    FINAL = "final"
    AMENDED = "amended"
    CORRECTED = "corrected"
    CANCELLED = "cancelled"
    ENTERED_IN_ERROR = "entered-in-error"
    UNKNOWN = "unknown"


class ReviewFlag(Enum):
    NONE = "none"
    PENDING_REVIEW = "pending-review"
    REVIEWED = "reviewed"


class PermissionError(Exception):
    """Raised when the caller lacks the required role."""


class ValidationError(Exception):
    """Raised when a mandatory element is missing or malformed."""


class StateTransitionError(Exception):
    """Raised when a status transition is not permitted."""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Vital-signs reference ranges (LOINC-keyed)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

VITAL_SIGNS_RANGES = {
    # LOINC code: (unit, min_plausible, max_plausible)
    "8867-4":  ("beats/min",  0,    300),    # Heart rate
    "9279-1":  ("breaths/min", 0,   100),    # Respiratory rate
    "8480-6":  ("mmHg",        0,   300),    # Systolic BP
    "8462-4":  ("mmHg",        0,   200),    # Diastolic BP
    "8310-5":  ("Cel",         25,   45),    # Body temperature
    "59408-5": ("%",           50,  100),    # Oxygen saturation
    "29463-7": ("kg",           0,  700),    # Body weight
    "8302-2":  ("cm",          40,  250),    # Body height
}

ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS = {
    ObservationStatus.REGISTERED:     {ObservationStatus.PRELIMINARY, ObservationStatus.FINAL},
    ObservationStatus.PRELIMINARY:    {ObservationStatus.FINAL, ObservationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR},
    ObservationStatus.FINAL:          {ObservationStatus.AMENDED, ObservationStatus.CORRECTED,
                                       ObservationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR},
    ObservationStatus.AMENDED:        {ObservationStatus.CORRECTED, ObservationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR},
    ObservationStatus.CORRECTED:      {ObservationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR},
    ObservationStatus.CANCELLED:      set(),
    ObservationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR: set(),
    ObservationStatus.UNKNOWN:        set(),
}

WRITE_ROLES = {"clinician", "nurse", "admin"}


@dataclass
class Quantity:
    value: float
    unit: str
    system: str = "http://unitsofmeasure.org"
    code: str = ""


@dataclass
class Coding:
    system: str
    code: str
    display: str = ""


@dataclass
class CodeableConcept:
    coding: list = field(default_factory=list)
    text: str = ""


@dataclass
class Observation:
    id: str
    status: ObservationStatus
    category: list                  # list[CodeableConcept]
    code: CodeableConcept
    subject_id: str
    value_quantity: Optional[Quantity] = None
    review_flag: ReviewFlag = ReviewFlag.NONE
    effective_date_time: Optional[str] = None
    performer_ids: list = field(default_factory=list)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Service under test
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class VitalSignsObservationService:
    """
    Validates and persists Observation resources that carry category
    'vital-signs'.  Out-of-range values are flagged for review rather
    than rejected, so downstream clinicians can act on them.
    """

    VITAL_SIGNS_CATEGORY_CODE = "vital-signs"
    VITAL_SIGNS_CATEGORY_SYSTEM = "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/observation-category"

    def __init__(self, repository, audit_logger, notifier):
        self._repo = repository
        self._audit = audit_logger
        self._notifier = notifier

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Internal helpers
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def _caller_has_write_permission(self, caller_role: str) -> bool:
        return caller_role in WRITE_ROLES

    def _is_vital_signs_category(self, observation: Observation) -> bool:
        for cat in observation.category:
            for coding in cat.coding:
                if (coding.system == self.VITAL_SIGNS_CATEGORY_SYSTEM
                        and coding.code == self.VITAL_SIGNS_CATEGORY_CODE):
                    return True
        return False

    def _validate_mandatory_fields(self, observation: Observation) -> None:
        errors = []
        if not observation.id:
            errors.append("id is required")
        if observation.status is None:
            errors.append("status is required")
        if not observation.category:
            errors.append("category is required")
        if not observation.code or not observation.code.coding:
            errors.append("code is required")
        if not observation.subject_id:
            errors.append("subject is required")
        if errors:
            raise ValidationError("; ".join(errors))

    def _get_loinc_code(self, observation: Observation) -> Optional[str]:
        for coding in observation.code.coding:
            if coding.system == "http://loinc.org":
                return coding.code
        return None

    def _check_range(self, observation: Observation) -> bool:
        """Returns True when value is within plausible range."""
        if observation.value_quantity is None:
            return True   # no value to check (e.g. component observations)
        loinc = self._get_loinc_code(observation)
        if loinc not in VITAL_SIGNS_RANGES:
            return True   # unknown code → cannot validate
        _unit, lo, hi = VITAL_SIGNS_RANGES[loinc]
        v = observation.value_quantity.value
        return lo <= v <= hi

    def _flag_for_review(self, observation: Observation) -> None:
        observation.review_flag = ReviewFlag.PENDING_REVIEW
        self._notifier.send_review_alert(observation)

    def _validate_transition(
        self,
        current_status: ObservationStatus,
        new_status: ObservationStatus,
    ) -> None:
        allowed = ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS.get(current_status, set())
        if new_status not in allowed:
            raise StateTransitionError(
                f"Transition {current_status.value} → {new_status.value} is not permitted"
            )

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Public API
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def create(self, observation: Observation, caller_role: str) -> Observation:
        if not self._caller_has_write_permission(caller_role):
            raise PermissionError(f"Role '{caller_role}' is not authorised to create observations")

        self._validate_mandatory_fields(observation)

        if self._is_vital_signs_category(observation):
            if not self._check_range(observation):
                self._flag_for_review(observation)

        self._repo.save(observation)
        self._audit.log("create", observation.id, caller_role)
        return observation

    def update_status(
        self,
        observation_id: str,
        new_status: ObservationStatus,
        caller_role: str,
    ) -> Observation:
        if not self._caller_has_write_permission(caller_role):
            raise PermissionError(f"Role '{caller_role}' is not authorised to update observations")

        obs = self._repo.find_by_id(observation_id)
        if obs is None:
            raise ValidationError(f"Observation {observation_id} not found")

        self._validate_transition(obs.status, new_status)
        obs.status = new_status
        self._repo.save(obs)
        self._audit.log("update_status", observation_id, caller_role)
        return obs


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture()
def mock_repo():
    repo = MagicMock()
    repo.find_by_id.return_value = None   # default: nothing stored
    return repo


@pytest.fixture()
def mock_audit():
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture()
def mock_notifier():
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture()
def service(mock_repo, mock_audit, mock_notifier):
    return VitalSignsObservationService(mock_repo, mock_audit, mock_notifier)


def _vital_signs_category() -> list:
    """Return a valid vital-signs category list."""
    return [
        CodeableConcept(
            coding=[
                Coding(
                    system="http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/observation-category",
                    code="vital-signs",
                    display="Vital Signs",
                )
            ]
        )
    ]


def _loinc_code(code: str, display: str = "") -> CodeableConcept:
    return CodeableConcept(
        coding=[Coding(system="http://loinc.org", code=code, display=display)]
    )


def _make_observation(
    obs_id: str = "obs-001",
    status: ObservationStatus = ObservationStatus.PRELIMINARY,
    loinc: str = "8867-4",          # heart rate
    value: Optional[float] = 72.0,
    unit: str = "beats/min",
    subject_id: str = "patient-123",
    category=None,
) -> Observation:
    vq = Quantity(value=value, unit=unit) if value is not None else None
    return Observation(
        id=obs_id,
        status=status,
        category=category if category is not None else _vital_signs_category(),
        code=_loinc_code(loinc),
        subject_id=subject_id,
        value_quantity=vq,
    )


# ===========================================================================
# 1. Happy-path: value inside plausible range
# ===========================================================================

class TestInRangeVitalSigns:

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("loinc,value,unit", [
        ("8867-4",  72,   "beats/min"),     # heart rate
        ("9279-1",  16,   "breaths/min"),   # respiratory rate
        ("8480-6",  120,  "mmHg"),          # systolic BP
        ("8462-4",  80,   "mmHg"),          # diastolic BP
        ("8310-5",  36.8, "Cel"),           # body temperature
        ("59408-5", 98,   "%"),             # SpO2
        ("29463-7", 70,   "kg"),            # body weight
        ("8302-2",  175,  "cm"),            # body height
    ])
    def test_in_range_value_accepted_without_flag(
        self, service, mock_repo, mock_notifier, loinc, value, unit
    ):
        obs = _make_observation(loinc=loinc, value=value, unit=unit)
        result = service.create(obs, caller_role="nurse")

        assert result.review_flag == ReviewFlag.NONE
        mock_repo.save.assert_called_once_with(obs)
        mock_notifier.send_review_alert.assert_not_called()

    def test_audit_logged_on_successful_create(self, service, mock_audit):
        obs = _make_observation()
        service.create(obs, caller_role="clinician")
        mock_audit.log.assert_called_once_with("create", obs.id, "clinician")

    def test_observation_returned_after_create(self, service):
        obs = _make_observation()
        result = service.create(obs, caller_role="nurse")
        assert result is obs

    def test_boundary_value_at_minimum_not_flagged(self, service, mock_notifier):
        # Heart rate = 0 (boundary; clinically unusual but within the plausible
        # lower bound used by the system)
        obs = _make_observation(loinc="8867-4", value=0, unit="beats/min")
        service.create(obs, caller_role="nurse")
        mock_notifier.send_review_alert.assert_not_called()

    def test_boundary_value_at_maximum_not_flagged(self, service, mock_notifier):
        obs = _make_observation(loinc="8867-4", value=300, unit="beats/min")
        service.create(obs, caller_role="nurse")
        mock_notifier.send_review_alert.assert_not_called()


# ===========================================================================
# 2. Out-of-range: flag for review
# ===========================================================================

class TestOutOfRangeVitalSigns:

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("loinc,value,unit", [
        ("8867-4",   -1,   "beats/min"),    # below 0
        ("8867-4",   301,  "beats/min"),    # above 300
        ("9279-1",   -5,   "breaths/min"),
        ("9279-1",   101,  "breaths/min"),
        ("8480-6",   -1,   "mmHg"),
        ("8480-6",   301,  "mmHg"),
        ("8310-5",   24.9, "Cel"),          # below 25
        ("8310-5",   45.1, "Cel"),          # above 45
        ("59408-5",  49,   "%"),            # below 50
        ("59408-5",  101,  "%"),            # above 100
        ("29463-7",  -0.1, "kg"),
        ("29463-7",  701,  "kg"),
        ("8302-2",   39,   "cm"),
        ("8302-2",   251,  "cm"),
    ])
    def test_out_of_range_value_flagged_for_review(
        self, service, mock_notifier, loinc, value, unit
    ):
        obs = _make_observation(loinc=loinc, value=value, unit=unit)
        result = service.create(obs, caller_role="nurse")

        assert result.review_flag == ReviewFlag.PENDING_REVIEW
        mock_notifier.send_review_alert.assert_called_once_with(obs)

    def test_out_of_range_observation_still_persisted(self, service, mock_repo):
        """Out-of-range MUST still be saved; only flagged, never silently dropped."""
        obs = _make_observation(loinc="8867-4", value=999, unit="beats/min")
        service.create(obs, caller_role="nurse")
        mock_repo.save.assert_called_once_with(obs)

    def test_audit_logged_even_for_out_of_range(self, service, mock_audit):
        obs = _make_observation(loinc="8867-4", value=999, unit="beats/min")
        service.create(obs, caller_role="nurse")
        mock_audit.log.assert_called_once_with("create", obs.id, "nurse")

    def test_review_alert_called_exactly_once_per_out_of_range(
        self, service, mock_notifier
    ):
        obs = _make_observation(loinc="8310-5", value=99, unit="Cel")
        service.create(obs, caller_role="clinician")
        assert mock_notifier.send_review_alert.call_count == 1

    def test_unknown_loinc_code_not_flagged(self, service, mock_notifier):
        """If the LOINC code has no reference range entry, skip flagging."""
        obs = _make_observation(loinc="99999-9", value=9999, unit="arbitrary")
        result = service.create(obs, caller_role="nurse")
        assert result.review_flag == ReviewFlag.NONE
        mock_notifier.send_review_alert.assert_not_called()

    def test_missing_value_quantity_not_flagged(self, service, mock_notifier):
        """Component observations without a top-level value are not range-checked."""
        obs = _make_observation(value=None)
        result = service.create(obs, caller_role="nurse")
        assert result.review_flag == ReviewFlag.NONE
        mock_notifier.send_review_alert.assert_not_called()

    def test_non_vital_signs_category_out_of_range_not_flagged(
        self, service, mock_notifier
    ):
        """Only observations with category=vital-signs are range-checked."""
        lab_category = [
            CodeableConcept(
                coding=[
                    Coding(
                        system="http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/observation-category",
                        code="laboratory",
                    )
                ]
            )
        ]
        obs = _make_observation(loinc="8867-4", value=999, unit="beats/min",
                                category=lab_category)
        result = service.create(obs, caller_role="nurse")
        assert result.review_flag == ReviewFlag.NONE
        mock_notifier.send_review_alert.assert_not_called()


# ===========================================================================
# 3. Permission denial (negative path)
# ===========================================================================

class TestPermissionDenial:

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("role", [
        "viewer", "patient", "auditor", "anonymous", "", "NURSE",  # case-sensitive
    ])
    def test_create_denied_for_unauthorised_role(self, service, role):
        obs = _make_observation()
        with pytest.raises(PermissionError) as exc_info:
            service.create(obs, caller_role=role)
        assert role in str(exc_info.value)

    def test_create_denied_does_not_persist(self, service, mock_repo):
        obs = _make_observation()
        with pytest.raises(PermissionError):
            service.create(obs, caller_role="viewer")
        mock_repo.save.assert_not_called()

    def test_create_denied_does_not_audit(self, service, mock_audit):
        obs = _make_observation()
        with pytest.raises(PermissionError):
            service.create(obs, caller_role="viewer")
        mock_audit.log.assert_not_called()

    def test_update_status_denied_for_unauthorised_role(
        self, service, mock_repo
    ):
        stored = _make_observation(status=ObservationStatus.REGISTERED)
        mock_repo.find_by_id.return_value = stored

        with pytest.raises(PermissionError):
            service.update_status(stored.id, ObservationStatus.FINAL, "viewer")

    def test_update_status_denied_does_not_mutate_stored_observation(
        self, service, mock_repo
    ):
        stored = _make_observation(status=ObservationStatus.REGISTERED)
        mock_repo.find_by_id.return_value = stored

        with pytest.raises(PermissionError):
            service.update_status(stored.id, ObservationStatus.FINAL, "viewer")

        mock_repo.save.assert_not_called()

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("role", ["clinician", "nurse", "admin"])
    def test_authorised_roles_can_create(self, service, role):
        obs = _make_observation()
        result = service.create(obs, caller_role=role)
        assert result is obs


# ===========================================================================
# 4. Missing mandatory elements (negative path)
# ===========================================================================

class TestMissingMandatoryElements:

    def test_missing_id_raises_validation_error(self, service):
        obs = _make_observation(obs_id="")
        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="id is required"):
            service.create(obs, caller_role="nurse")

    def test_missing_subject_raises_validation_error(self, service):
        obs = _make_observation(subject_id="")
        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="subject is required"):
            service.create(obs, caller_role="nurse")

    def test_missing_category_raises_validation_error(self, service):
        obs = _make_observation(category=[])
        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="category is required"):
            service.create(obs, caller_role="nurse")

    def test_missing_code_raises_validation_error(self, service):
        obs = _make_observation()
        obs.code = CodeableConcept(coding=[])   # empty coding list
        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="code is required"):
            service.create(obs, caller_role="nurse")

    def test_missing_status_raises_validation_error(self, service):
        obs = _make_observation()
        obs.status = None
        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="status is required"):
            service.create(obs, caller_role="nurse")

    def test_multiple_missing_fields_all_reported(self, service):
        obs = _make_observation(obs_id="", subject_id="")
        with pytest.raises(ValidationError) as exc_info:
            service.create(obs, caller_role="nurse")
        msg = str(exc_info.value)
        assert "id is required" in msg
        assert "subject is required" in msg

    def test_validation_error_prevents_save(self, service, mock_repo):
        obs = _make_observation(obs_id="")
        with pytest.raises(ValidationError):
            service.create(obs, caller_role="nurse")
        mock_repo.save.assert_not_called()

    def test_validation_error_prevents_audit(self, service, mock_audit):
        obs = _make_observation(obs_id="")
        with pytest.raises(ValidationError):
            service.create(obs, caller_role="nurse")
        mock_audit.log.assert_not_called()

    def test_update_status_for_nonexistent_observation(self, service, mock_repo):
        mock_repo.find_by_id.return_value = None
        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="not found"):
            service.update_status("ghost-id", ObservationStatus.FINAL, "clinician")


# ===========================================================================
# 5. Conflicting state transitions (negative path)
# ===========================================================================

class TestConflictingStateTransitions:

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("from_status,to_status", [
        # Terminal → anything
        (ObservationStatus.CANCELLED,        ObservationStatus.FINAL),
        (ObservationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR, ObservationStatus.FINAL),
        (ObservationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR, ObservationStatus.PRELIMINARY),
        (ObservationStatus.UNKNOWN,          ObservationStatus.FINAL),
        # Skipping intermediate states
        (ObservationStatus.REGISTERED,       ObservationStatus.AMENDED),
        (ObservationStatus.REGISTERED,       ObservationStatus.CORRECTED),
        (ObservationStatus.PRELIMINARY,      ObservationStatus.CORRECTED),
        # Backwards transitions
        (ObservationStatus.FINAL,            ObservationStatus.PRELIMINARY),
        (ObservationStatus.FINAL,            ObservationStatus.REGISTERED),
        (ObservationStatus.AMENDED,          ObservationStatus.FINAL),
        (ObservationStatus.CORRECTED,        ObservationStatus.AMENDED),
        # Self-transition
        (ObservationStatus.FINAL,            ObservationStatus.FINAL),
        (ObservationStatus.PRELIMINARY,      ObservationStatus.PRELIMINARY),
    ])
    def test_invalid_transition_raises_state_transition_error(
        self, service, mock_repo, from_status, to_status
    ):
        stored = _make_observation(status=from_status)
        mock_repo.find_by_id.return_value = stored

        with pytest.raises(StateTransitionError):
            service.update_status(stored.id, to_status, "clinician")

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("from_status,to_status", [
        (ObservationStatus.REGISTERED,  ObservationStatus.PRELIMINARY),
        (ObservationStatus.REGISTERED,  ObservationStatus.FINAL),
        (ObservationStatus.PRELIMINARY, ObservationStatus.FINAL),
        (ObservationStatus.PRELIMINARY, ObservationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR),
        (ObservationStatus.FINAL,       ObservationStatus.AMENDED),
        (ObservationStatus.FINAL,       ObservationStatus.CORRECTED),
        (ObservationStatus.FINAL,       ObservationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR),
        (ObservationStatus.AMENDED,     ObservationStatus.CORRECTED),
        (ObservationStatus.AMENDED,     ObservationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR),
        (ObservationStatus.CORRECTED,   ObservationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR),
    ])
    def test_valid_transitions_succeed(
        self, service, mock_repo, from_status, to_status
    ):
        stored = _make_observation(status=from_status)
        mock_repo.find_by_id.return_value = stored

        result = service.update_status(stored.id, to_status, "clinician")
        assert result.status == to_status

    def test_invalid_transition_does_not_persist_new_status(
        self, service, mock_repo
    ):
        stored = _make_observation(status=ObservationStatus.CANCELLED)
        mock_repo.find_by_id.return_value = stored

        with pytest.raises(StateTransitionError):
            service.update_status(stored.id, ObservationStatus.FINAL, "clinician")

        mock_repo.save.assert_not_called()

    def test_invalid_transition_does_not_mutate_in_memory_status(
        self, service, mock_repo
    ):
        stored = _make_observation(status=ObservationStatus.CANCELLED)
        mock_repo.find_by_id.return_value = stored

        with pytest.raises(StateTransitionError):
            service.update_status(stored.id, ObservationStatus.FINAL, "clinician")

        assert stored.status == ObservationStatus.CANCELLED

    def test_invalid_transition_error_message_contains_both_states(
        self, service, mock_repo
    ):
        stored = _make_observation(status=ObservationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR)
        mock_repo.find_by_id.return_value = stored

        with pytest.raises(StateTransitionError) as exc_info:
            service.update_status(stored.id, ObservationStatus.FINAL, "clinician")

        msg = str(exc_info.value)
        assert "entered-in-error" in msg
        assert "final" in msg

    def test_valid_transition_audit_logged(self, service, mock_repo, mock_audit):
        stored = _make_observation(status=ObservationStatus.REGISTERED)
        mock_repo.find_by_id.return_value = stored

        service.update_status(stored.id, ObservationStatus.FINAL, "clinician")
        mock_audit.log.assert_called_once_with(
            "update_status", stored.id, "clinician"
        )


# ===========================================================================
# 6. Interaction / integration-style tests
# ===========================================================================

class TestCombinedScenarios:

    def test_out_of_range_then_review_flag_then_status_update(
        self, service, mock_repo, mock_notifier
    ):
        """End-to-end: create out-of-range obs, check flag, transition to final."""
        obs = _make_observation(loinc="8867-4", value=350, unit="beats/min",
                                status=ObservationStatus.PRELIMINARY)
        service.create(obs, caller_role="nurse")

        assert obs.review_flag == ReviewFlag.PENDING_REVIEW
        mock_notifier.send_review_alert.assert_called_once()

        # Simulate the repository returning the (now saved) observation
        mock_repo.find_by_id.return_value = obs
        updated = service.update_status(obs.id, ObservationStatus.FINAL, "clinician")
        assert updated.status == ObservationStatus.FINAL

    def test_permission_check_takes_priority_over_validation(
        self, service, mock_repo
    ):
        """Permission denial should be raised before field validation."""
        obs = _make_observation(obs_id="")   # also has a validation error
        with pytest.raises(PermissionError):
            service.create(obs, caller_role="viewer")
        mock_repo.save.assert_not_called()

    def test_multiple_observations_independent_flags(
        self, service, mock_notifier
    ):
        obs_in  = _make_observation(obs_id="obs-1", value=72)
        obs_out = _make_observation(obs_id="obs-2", value=999)

        service.create(obs_in,  caller_role="nurse")
        service.create(obs_out, caller_role="nurse")

        assert obs_in.review_flag  == ReviewFlag.NONE
        assert obs_out.review_flag == ReviewFlag.PENDING_REVIEW
        assert mock_notifier.send_review_alert.call_count == 1

    def test_repository_save_called_with_correct_observation_object(
        self, service, mock_repo
    ):
        obs = _make_observation()
        service.create(obs, caller_role="admin")
        mock_repo.save.assert_called_once_with(obs)

    def test_notifier_receives_flagged_observation_not_a_copy(
        self, service, mock_notifier
    ):
        obs = _make_observation(loinc="8867-4", value=-10, unit="beats/min")
        service.create(obs, caller_role="nurse")
        notified_obs = mock_notifier.send_review_alert.call_args[0][0]
        assert notified_obs is obs
        assert notified_obs.review_flag == ReviewFlag.PENDING_REVIEW
```

---

## Design notes

| Concern | Decision |
|---|---|
| **Test isolation** | Every external collaborator (`repository`, `audit_logger`, `notifier`) is a `MagicMock`; no real I/O occurs. |
| **Parameterisation** | `@pytest.mark.parametrize` covers all eight LOINC vital-sign codes for in-range and out-of-range paths, and all transition pairs for valid/invalid status

"""
pytest tests for FHIR Observation vital-signs range validation.

Requirement:
    An Observation with category vital-signs shall have value in a clinically
    plausible range; out-of-range values shall be flagged for review.
    Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements,
    and conflicting state transitions must be tested.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from enum import Enum
from typing import Optional

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models (production code would live elsewhere; defined here so the
# test file is self-contained and runnable with `pytest` directly)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class ObservationStatus(Enum):
    REGISTERED = "registered"
    PRELIMINARY = "preliminary"
    FINAL = "final"
    AMENDED = "amended"
    CORRECTED = "corrected"
    CANCELLED = "cancelled"
    ENTERED_IN_ERROR = "entered-in-error"
    UNKNOWN = "unknown"


class ReviewFlag(Enum):
    NONE = "none"
    PENDING_REVIEW = "pending-review"
    REVIEWED = "reviewed"


class PermissionError(Exception):
    """Raised when the caller lacks the required role."""


class ValidationError(Exception):
    """Raised when a mandatory element is missing or malformed."""


class StateTransitionError(Exception):
    """Raised when a status transition is not permitted."""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Vital-signs reference ranges (LOINC-keyed)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

"""

Requirement:
    An Observation with category vital-signs shall have value in a clinically

    Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements,
    and conflicting state transitions must be tested.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from enum import Enum
from typing import Optional

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models (production code would live elsewhere; defined here so the
# test file is self-contained and runnable with `pytest` directly)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class ObservationStatus(Enum):
    REGISTERED = "registered"
    PRELIMINARY = "preliminary"
    FINAL = "final"
    AMENDED = "amended"
    CORRECTED = "corrected"
    CANCELLED = "cancelled"
    ENTERED_IN_ERROR = "entered-in-error"
    UNKNOWN = "unknown"


class ReviewFlag(Enum):
    NONE = "none"
    PENDING_REVIEW = "pending-review"
    REVIEWED = "reviewed"


class PermissionError(Exception):
    """Raised when the caller lacks the required role."""


class ValidationError(Exception):
    """Raised when a mandatory element is missing or malformed."""


class StateTransitionError(Exception):
    """Raised when a status transition is not permitted."""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Vital-signs reference ranges (LOINC-keyed)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

VITAL_SIGNS_RANGES = {
    # LOINC code: (unit, min_plausible, max_plausible)
    "8867-4":  ("beats/min",  0,    300),    # Heart rate
    "9279-1":  ("breaths/min", 0,   100),    # Respiratory rate
    "8480-6":  ("mmHg",        0,   300),    # Systolic BP
    "8462-4":  ("mmHg",        0,   200),    # Diastolic BP
    "8310-5":  ("Cel",         25,   45),    # Body temperature
    "59408-5": ("%",           50,  100),    # Oxygen saturation
    "29463-7": ("kg",           0,  700),    # Body weight
    "8302-2":  ("cm",          40,  250),    # Body height
}

ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS = {
    ObservationStatus.REGISTERED:     {ObservationStatus.PRELIMINARY, ObservationStatus.FINAL},
    ObservationStatus.PRELIMINARY:    {ObservationStatus.FINAL, ObservationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR},
    ObservationStatus.FINAL:          {ObservationStatus.AMENDED, ObservationStatus.CORRECTED,
                                       ObservationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR},
    ObservationStatus.AMENDED:        {ObservationStatus.CORRECTED, ObservationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR},
    ObservationStatus.CORRECTED:      {ObservationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR},
    ObservationStatus.CANCELLED:      set(),
    ObservationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR: set(),
    ObservationStatus.UNKNOWN:        set(),
}

WRITE_ROLES = {"clinician", "nurse", "admin"}


@dataclass
class Quantity:
    value: float
    unit: str
    system: str = "http://unitsofmeasure.org"
    code: str = ""


@dataclass
class Coding:
    system: str
    code: str
    display: str = ""


@dataclass
class CodeableConcept:
    coding: list = field(default_factory=list)
    text: str = ""


@dataclass
class Observation:
    id: str
    status: ObservationStatus
    category: list                  # list[CodeableConcept]
    code: CodeableConcept
    subject_id: str
    value_quantity: Optional[Quantity] = None
    review_flag: ReviewFlag = ReviewFlag.NONE
    effective_date_time: Optional[str] = None
    performer_ids: list = field(default_factory=list)
